desc: process_items appends to a module-global results list, so results from prior calls leak into later calls.
Every process_items call returns the same mutable global list, so previously returned outputs change when later calls append results.
expensive_compute constructs and evaluates source code from x, allowing code execution when an attacker-controlled object supplies a malicious string representation.
expensive_compute catches every Exception and returns 0, concealing programming errors and making failures indistinguishable from valid results.
expensive_compute returns numbers, None, or the string "invalid", forcing callers to handle an undocumented heterogeneous result type.
get_user_data converts input to a string before lookup, so textual input such as "1" cannot retrieve a result cached under numeric key 1.
The cache and results collections grow without eviction or reset, causing unbounded memory consumption in a long-running process.
process_items uses a shared mutable list as its default argument, making future mutation or refactoring prone to cross-call state leakage.
Unsynchronized reads and writes to the global cache and results list can produce duplicate computation and nondeterministic behavior under concurrent calls.
process_items sleeps for every item regardless of need, imposing avoidable latency and making the function harder to test and reuse.
Wrapping results.append in a one-element list creates and immediately discards an unnecessary list on every iteration.
